# Pre-work Intake Form (Customer-friendly)

**Instructions:** Ideally completed asynchronously before the first discovery call.

## Goal
Give the team enough context to run a productive first discovery conversation without turning this form into a long requirements exercise.

## When to use
- Before the first serious discovery call
- When Sales needs a lightweight qualification artifact
- When the customer needs a simple form they can complete without technical deep dives

## Owner
Sales / Account Executive requests and reviews it; the customer completes it.

## Inputs required
- A basic understanding of the business problem
- Access to anyone who can answer stakeholder or timeline questions
- Real examples of the current workflow if available

## Who should complete it
The executive sponsor / business sponsor or customer product owner / business owner. They can ask IT or SMEs for help on specific questions if needed.

## What this should produce
- A clear problem statement and urgency signal
- Named stakeholders for the next sessions
- An early view of workflows, scope, and known constraints

## Timebox
10-15 minutes for a first pass.

## 1) Business problem, urgency, and outcomes
- What problem/opportunity are you addressing (1–2 sentences)?
- Why now? What happens if this is delayed for 6–12 months?
- What outcomes matter (time saved, cost reduced, revenue, risk reduction)?
- How will you measure success 90 days after go-live?

## 2) Users, workflows, and rough scale
- Who are the primary user roles?
- What are the top 3 tasks they must complete end-to-end?
- Roughly how many users, teams, or business units are involved?

## 3) Current state and pain points
- What tools/processes do you use today?
- Where does the process fail (delays, errors, compliance risk)?
- What is most manual, repetitive, or frustrating today?

## 4) Scope signal
- If you had to launch quickly, what must work in version 1?
- What can wait until later?

## 5) Systems and constraints
- What systems/data does this touch (ERP/CRM/Identity/files/data warehouse)?
- Any hard constraints (SSO, data residency, on-prem only, etc.)?
- Any integration, approval, or compliance constraints already known?

## 6) Stakeholders and buying process
- Who is the executive sponsor / business sponsor?
- Who is the day-to-day product owner / business owner?
- Which SMEs / power users should join later workshops?
- Which IT, security, or procurement contacts should be involved?
- Who approves purchase? Who can block it (IT, Security, Procurement)?
- Do you have a vendor onboarding or security review process? If yes, when does it usually happen?

## 7) Timeline and decision points
- Target go-live date and what drives it?
- Is there a key internal decision date before go-live?
- Are there any milestones or events we should plan around?

## Helpful evidence before the call (if available)
- 1–3 screenshots or sample docs from the current workflow
- Any existing process description, SOP, or slide deck

## Helpful evidence later if requested
- Sample data export or a dummy example record
- List of systems involved and technical owners (names/emails)
- Security or procurement onboarding forms
